summarization.a: The figure shows the deposition rate on various materials (Si, Al2O3, HfO2, AlNx, TiN, SiO2) against the fraction of CF4, with the Ar fraction kept constant at 83.3%. Negative values indicate etching.
summarization.b: The figure illustrates the concentration of F, CF2, and CF species over the fraction of CF4, with the Ar fraction kept constant at 83.3%

a.question: Which two materials show etching rather than deposition at high CF₄ fractions?
Based on the deposition behavior, which CF₄ fraction regime would be optimal for the ALE modification step and why?
a.answer: Low CF₄ fraction (<5%) because: (1) provides polymer deposition on target materials (Al₂O₃, AlNx, HfO₂), (2) avoids etching of potential mask materials (SiO₂, TiN)

a.question: Do all materials show maximum deposition rate at the same CF₄ fraction?

b.question: Which radical species shows the highest concentration at 13.3% CF₄?
